name: Ralph MacDonald
description: Trinbagonian-American percussionist, songwriter, musical arranger, record producer, steelpan virtuoso and philanthropist
abstract: Ralph Anthony MacDonald (March 15, 1944 – December 18, 2011) was an American percussionist, steelpan virtuoso, songwriter, musical arranger, and record producer. His compositions include "Where Is the Love", a Grammy Award winner for the duet of Roberta Flack and Donny Hathaway; "Just the Two of Us", recorded by Bill Withers and Grover W…

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: There’s a record called “The Path” by Ralph McDonald. And that has a really good example where he purposely called me, Mike, Sanborn, Barry... And we did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Hank Cicalo
about_quotes.evidence: The A bands in New York for a long time was Richard Tee, Steve Gadd on drums, Ralph McDonald percussion, I mean Hugh McCracken on guitar, Eric Gale on guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hank Cicalo interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 30, 2022
